goal: Complete the quarterly pressure audit on schedule
conflict: Anomalous readings in eastern sector don't match any known pattern
crisis: Report the anomaly and risk protocol review, or file it as instrument error and stay on schedule
decision: Files as instrument error but keeps a private note
knowledge_in: Standard pressure maps are reliable; eastern sector is stable
knowledge_out: Eastern readings don't match; has private note of anomaly
key_actions: Reviews maps;Finds anomaly;Consults Tessa;Files as error;Makes private note
key_dialogue: The eastern readings are within acceptable variance;Dorren privately: "Acceptable is not the same as explained"

goal: Understand the anomaly by cross-referencing historical archives
conflict: The village that should appear on the 40-year map is simply absent — no record of removal
crisis: Accept the archive is incomplete and move on, or pursue an explanation that has no institutional support
decision: Decides to pursue quietly, outside protocol
knowledge_out: A village has been erased from the maps; the erasure is systematic
key_actions: Cross-references maps;Discovers missing village;Searches removal logs;Finds nothing
key_dialogue: It was there forty years ago. It isn't there now. There is no note.

goal: Convince the council that the eastern anomalies require investigation
conflict: Council members see the anomaly as Dorren's procedural fixation, not evidence of erasure
crisis: Accept the council's dismissal and work within channels, or go outside the institution entirely
decision: Goes outside — shares findings with Kael privately after the meeting
knowledge_in: Village erasure is systematic; archive shows inconsistencies
knowledge_out: Council will not act; must work outside institution
key_actions: Presents evidence;Council dismisses;Dorren argues;Is overruled;Meets Kael after
key_dialogue: Council: "We appreciate your diligence, Cartographer.";Dorren to Kael: "They won't look."
